sentence: This book is more interesting than that one
hint: Subject + is + more + adjective + than + object
explanation: Comparatives with long adjectives use <more ... than>.

sentence: She is taller than her brother
explanation: Short adjectives add <-er> for comparatives: <taller than>.

sentence: This is the most expensive restaurant in the city
hint: Subject + is + the most + adjective + noun + place
explanation: Superlatives with long adjectives use <the most>.

sentence: He is the fastest runner in the school
hint: Subject + is + the + adjective-est + noun + place
explanation: Short adjectives add <-est> for superlatives: <the fastest>.

sentence: My house is bigger than yours
hint: Possessive + noun + is + adjective-er + than + pronoun
explanation: Short adjectives double the consonant and add <-er>: <bigger>.

sentence: This is the best pizza I have ever eaten
hint: Subject + is + the best + noun + present perfect
explanation: <Best> is the superlative of <good>. We often use it with present perfect.
Short adjectives double the consonant and add <-er>: <hotter>.

sentence: She is the most beautiful girl in the class
hint: Subject + is + the most + adjective + noun + place
explanation: Long adjectives use <the most> for superlatives.

sentence: This exercise is less difficult than the last one
hint: Subject + is + less + adjective + than + object
explanation: Use <less ... than> for the opposite of <more ... than>.

sentence: My cat is lazier than my dog
hint: Possessive + noun + is + adjective-er + than + possessive + noun

sentence: That was the worst film I have ever seen
hint: Subject + was + the worst + noun + present perfect
